title: Dropping default value definitions from a table does not work as expected
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER IDENTITY PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50) DEFAULT 'unknown', age INTEGER DEFAULT 18, created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (name, age, created_at) VALUES ('unknown', 18, CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
ALTER TABLE test_table ALTER COLUMN name SET DEFAULT NULL;
ALTER TABLE test_table ALTER COLUMN age SET DEFAULT NULL;
key_bug_features: Dropping default values from table columns fails
